issue_title: EventSubscriber beforeInsert, afterInsert missing data for junctionTables
issue_body:
### Issue description

The EventSubscriber's beforeInsert, afterInser, methods are called for junction tables. But there is no way to get the data that is inserted 

### Expected Behavior

When a many-to-many-relationship is inserted, the EventSubscriber's functions are called. Inside this functions there shuld be a way to get the data that is inserted or removed. Normally this would be via the `entityId`  object like in `beforeRemove` and `afterRemove`. But in the case of a junction-table this is not defined and `entity` is undefined. 

I'm working on a synchronisation-library that has a mysql-server-database and a sqljs-client one. The basic idea is to execute the query locally, send the query to the server together with a `last executed`-timestamp and execute the query there again with only selecting the changed entities since the `last executed`-timestamp. For this every model has an `updatedAt` column, which is compared at database-level. 

The `updatedAt` column is updated through an EventSubscriber.
if a Many-to-Many relation is updated, the EventSubscriber for the saving entity is not called, as described in https://github.com/typeorm/typeorm/issues/412 which is fine. I want to get the two entities of the relation that is saved in the junctionTable, that triggers the EventSubscriber's function and update the `updatedAt` manually from the EventSubscriber for those two. But alas the referenced primary keys are not accessible inside the EventSubscriber's function. From the metadata send inside the event I can get the corresponding EntityClasses and Tables inside the database, but not the entities.

### Actual Behavior

The values should be somehow accessible inside the EventSubscriber's functions. Prefferably via the `entity` or `entityId` object.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add an EventSubscriber which listen to all models
2. Change and save a many-to-many relation with a junction table
3. event.entity inside the EventSubscriber's beforeInsert and afterInsert functions for the junction table is undefined and there is no way of getting the saving data

### My Environment

| Dependency          | Version  |
| ---                 | ---      |
| Operating System    |  Linux        |
| Node.js version     | 16.20.0  |
| Typescript version  | 4.6.3  |
| TypeORM version     |  0.3.14 (because of https://github.com/typeorm/typeorm/issues/9959 not 0.3.15) |


### Additional Context

_No response_

### Relevant Database Driver(s)

- [ ] aurora-mysql
- [ ] aurora-postgres
- [ ] better-sqlite3
- [ ] cockroachdb
- [ ] cordova
- [ ] expo
- [ ] mongodb
- [X] mysql
- [ ] nativescript
- [ ] oracle
- [ ] postgres
- [ ] react-native
- [ ] sap
- [ ] spanner
- [ ] sqlite
- [ ] sqlite-abstract
- [ ] sqljs
- [ ] sqlserver

### Are you willing to resolve this issue by submitting a Pull Request?

Yes, I have the time, but I don't know how to start. I would need guidance.
pr_title: fix: resolve issue EventSubscribers beforeInsert and afterInsert missing data for junction tables
pr_body:
<!--
  😀 Wonderful!  Thank you for opening a pull request for TypeORM.

  https://github.com/typeorm/typeorm/blob/master/docs/support.md
-->

### Description of change

Fixes #9974


When data is inserted into a junctionTable (through updating Many-To-Many-Relations) the EventSubscriber's beforeInsert and afterInsert methods are called, but without data. This MR changes that.
